metadata.narrative_title: Lore collapse, Shield persistence, and the decisionist substitute

nodes.label: Lore Capacity
nodes.description: The institutional capacity to produce, transmit, and metabolise shared narrative — the press, the academy, civic religion, the bipartisan consensus apparatus, the common interpretive framework that turns events into shared meaning. In the 2026 reading this stock has fallen to Node Value 0.6, the lowest in 127 years of US data. Lore Capacity is the substrate of democratic deliberation; without it, deliberation becomes performance and coercion is what is left. [n1]

nodes.label: Helm Coherence
nodes.description: The executive function's capacity to operate coherently within and through the rest of the institutional architecture: working with Stewards, respecting Archive, addressing Hands through Lore. In the 2026 reading Helm sits at 0.4, the second-lowest in the dataset. A degraded Helm does not stop governing — it governs differently, through decisionist symbolic acts rather than through institutional process. [n2]

nodes.label: Shield Authority
nodes.description: The security and enforcement apparatus broadly understood: federal agencies with coercive powers, the surveillance infrastructure, the federalised police functions, the legally privileged spaces of national-security exception. In 2026 Shield holds at 10.7 — seventeen times the Lore reading. Shield is the only institutional node currently operating at near-historical norms, which means it now defines reality by default rather than by mandate. [n3]

nodes.label: Archive Stock
nodes.description: The inherited legal-institutional memory: the Constitution, the federal courts, the surviving regulatory and professional norms, the legacy institutions still operating on the older legitimacy. At Node Value 6.0 in 2026, Archive is the country's highest-functioning institutional capacity after Shield, and is the working capital the entire system is currently drawing down. Archive can be drawn down faster than it can be replenished. [n4]

nodes.label: Mass Availability
nodes.description: The structural state of the Hands node when Lore is absent: a stressed, narratively unanchored population that has been told for thirty years that the institutions which used to interpret its experience now despise it. Available Mass is not captured mass — it is the precondition for capture. The current reading (Hands at 4.2, Stress at 7.0) describes a mass that is structurally susceptible to whichever coherence-offer arrives first with sufficient confidence. [n5]

nodes.label: Symbolic Acts
nodes.description: Mass pardons, executive orders that exceed statutory scope, dismissals of inspectors general, federal deployments into cities, agency reorganisations away from statutory missions. Each is unappealable, immediate, and addressed past the institutional intermediaries to the mass directly. Symbolic Acts function as emergency Lore injections — concentrated assertions of story when ordinary narrative channels can no longer carry one. [n6]

nodes.label: Coherence Demand
nodes.description: The latent population-level demand for a working interpretive framework: an account of who we are, what is happening, what is right and what is wrong. Coherence Demand rises with Mass Stress and falls when Lore Capacity is delivering working narrative. When Lore fails and Stress rises, Coherence Demand becomes the energy that whichever coherence-offer can capture. [n7]

nodes.label: Decisionist Authority
nodes.description: The emergent capacity of the executive to govern through symbolic act rather than institutional process. Decisionist Authority rises when Helm cannot govern through ordinary channels and Symbolic Acts begin filling the gap. It is a real form of authority that produces real political effects, but it does not rebuild the institutional substrate it bypasses. The substitution is one-directional. [n8]

nodes.label: Praetorian Reach
nodes.description: The expanding scope of Shield activity into domains previously governed by Helm, Lore, Stewards, or Archive: federal agents in city policing, surveillance in domestic political life, security exceptions in regulatory processes, military presence in civilian contexts. Praetorian Reach grows when other nodes fail and recedes only when they recover — which they cannot do while Shield is performing their functions for them. [n9]

nodes.label: Counter-Narrative Mobilisation
nodes.description: The reactive coherence-offers arising in opposition to Decisionist Authority: legal mobilisation through the courts, oppositional media framings, protest movements, alternative civic projects. Each is rational in its own terms. Collectively they intensify the politicisation of the Archive (because they fight through it) and the fragmentation of Lore (because they replace shared narrative with competing partial ones). [n10]

nodes.label: External Shock Pressure
nodes.description: Acute disruptions from outside the system: economic shocks, foreign-policy crises, climate events, technological disruptions, pandemic-class biological events. Shocks are exogenous in origin but their cost to the system depends entirely on the institutional capacity available when they arrive. The current configuration metabolises shocks poorly because the metabolising institutions are precisely the ones that have collapsed. [n11]

nodes.label: Civic Renewal Capacity
nodes.description: The latent capacity of citizens, in plural and deliberately, to undertake the patient work of rebuilding the institutional substrate that decisionism has been substituting for. This is the leverage variable of the entire system. Renewal cannot be performed by any administration, any party, or the security apparatus. It can only be performed by civic actors operating outside the partisan logic that structures every other actor's choices. [n12]

edges.label:
Lore satisfies coherence demand
−
Lag: Years
edges.description: Working Lore Capacity directly reduces Coherence Demand by supplying the interpretive framework the population uses to metabolise events. When Lore is functioning, demand is low because supply is meeting it. When Lore collapses, demand does not disappear — it becomes available to whichever alternative supply arrives first. The link is the foundational health-of-deliberation measure of the entire diagram. [e1]

edges.label:
Lore supports executive function
+
Lag: Years
edges.description: Helm coherence depends on Lore Capacity: an executive that can speak through a working narrative apparatus governs differently from one that cannot. When Lore collapses, Helm loses its primary interface with the population and is forced into decisionist substitutes. The historical pattern across the dataset is that Helm declines follow Lore declines with a lag of three to seven years. [e2]

edges.label:
Strong Helm reduces decree-based action
−
Lag: Months
edges.description: A functioning Helm operating through institutional process has less need to govern by Symbolic Act. The negative polarity reflects the inverse relation: as Helm Coherence degrades, the executive's available toolkit narrows toward symbolic decree, and the frequency and scope of Symbolic Acts rises. This is the structural pressure producing the contemporary decree-heavy governance pattern. [e3]

edges.label:
Demand pulls symbolic supply
+
Lag: Months
edges.description: Unmet Coherence Demand creates direct political pressure for Symbolic Acts: the population's narrative hunger pulls decisionist supply from the executive. This is the demand-side pressure behind the contemporary pattern of mass pardons, executive orders, and high-visibility federal interventions. The acts function because the demand is present, not because the underlying institutional capacity has been restored. [e4]

edges.label:
Symbolic acts temporarily relieve demand
−
Lag: Weeks
edges.description: Symbolic Acts do reduce Coherence Demand in the short term: the mass pardon, the dramatic executive order, the federal deployment each provide a brief surge of felt coherence that absorbs the demand pressure. The relief is real and is the reason the mechanism functions politically. The relief is also short-lived and does not address the underlying Lore Capacity that produced the demand. [e5]

edges.label:
Acts build decisionist authority
+
Lag: Months
edges.description: Each Symbolic Act successfully delivered increases the structural Decisionist Authority of the executive: the demonstrated capacity to govern past institutional intermediaries directly to the mass. This authority is real and compounds with use. It is also a different kind of authority from the rule-based legitimacy it is replacing, and the substitution is one-directional. [e6]

edges.label:
Decisionism erodes narrative substrate
−
Lag: Years
edges.description: Growing Decisionist Authority further erodes Lore Capacity by making the institutional channels of narrative production look slow, compromised, and unnecessary by comparison. Why wait for the press, the courts, the deliberative process when the executive can simply declare? Each successful decisionist act demonstrates that the slow channels are dispensable, accelerating their atrophy. [e7]

edges.label:
Decisionism draws down Archive
−
Lag: Years
edges.description: Decisionist Authority operates by drawing on Archive legitimacy without replenishing it: the constitutional framework, the institutional precedent, the inherited norms supply the legitimacy capital that the symbolic acts spend. Each draw is rational individually. The cumulative draw across many acts depletes the Archive at a rate the institution cannot regenerate, which is the deep cost of decisionist governance. [e8]

edges.label:
Symbolic acts expand Shield scope
+
Lag: Months
edges.description: Symbolic Acts frequently involve or invoke the security apparatus: deployments, dismissals, surveillance authorities, enforcement priorities. Each expansion increases Praetorian Reach into domains previously governed by other nodes. The expansion is rarely formally reversed even when the immediate act ends, because the operational capacity built for it persists. [e9]

edges.label:
Shield capacity enables reach
+
Lag: Months
edges.description: Shield Authority's structural strength (NV 10.7 in 2026) provides the operational capacity that Praetorian Reach exercises. A weaker Shield could not perform the expanded functions even when the political environment created demand for them. The high Shield value is what makes the Praetorian configuration practically available rather than merely theoretical. [e10]

edges.label:
Praetorian reach suppresses Lore renewal
−
Lag: Years
edges.description: Expanded Praetorian Reach actively suppresses the conditions under which Lore Capacity could rebuild: chilled press, surveilled civic organisations, security-vetted academic environments, normalised exception-spaces. The chilling effect operates without explicit censorship — the operational presence of the security apparatus in narrative-producing spaces is sufficient to shift what gets said, by whom, with what confidence. [e11]

edges.label:
Praetorian reach atrophies Helm
−
Lag: Years
edges.description: When the security apparatus performs functions that should belong to Helm — interpretation, deterrence, coordination — the executive function atrophies in those domains. Helm is a use-it-or-lose-it capacity. The longer Shield substitutes for it, the less Helm can be restored to those functions when the political environment eventually permits it. [e12]

edges.label:
Mass availability magnifies demand
+
Lag: Months
edges.description: A stressed, narratively unanchored mass produces sustained high Coherence Demand: the structural condition of Mass Availability is precisely the condition of unmet narrative need. The demand is not adaptive — it is symptomatic — and the longer it goes unmet by legitimate Lore, the more readily it is captured by illegitimate alternatives. [e13]

edges.label:
Symbolic acts capture mass briefly
+
Lag: Weeks
edges.description: Successful Symbolic Acts produce visible alignment of segments of the mass with the executive: rallies, polling shifts, identity confirmation. The capture is real but partial and unstable — each act captures a subset of the available mass and leaves others unaffected or repelled, producing the oscillating fragmentary cohesion the dataset registers. [e14]

edges.label:
Availability also fuels opposition
+
Lag: Months
edges.description: Mass Availability is not directional: the same narrative hunger that pulls Symbolic Acts from the executive also pulls Counter-Narrative Mobilisation from the opposition. The mass available for capture by decisionist authority is also the mass available for capture by oppositional movements, which is why the contemporary period registers as polarised rather than dominated by a single capture. [e15]

edges.label:
Counter-mobilisation also draws Archive
−
Lag: Years
edges.description: Opposition mobilisation through the courts, the regulatory state, and the institutional press also draws on Archive legitimacy without replenishing it: each lawsuit, each injunction, each impeachment proceeding spends institutional capital. The opposition is fighting for the right values through the available channels and the cumulative effect of those channels being used as partisan battlefields is the further politicisation of the Archive itself. [e16]

edges.label:
Counter-narratives fragment Lore
−
Lag: Years
edges.description: Counter-Narrative Mobilisation, by producing oppositional partial narratives where shared Lore used to exist, further fragments the country's interpretive substrate. Each side speaks past the other, with the result that the institutional capacity for cross-cutting consensus continues to degrade even when the substance of one side's claims is correct. The structural cost is independent of the substantive merit. [e17]

edges.label:
Archive frames executive scope
+
Lag: Years
edges.description: Archive Stock — operating constitutional norms, surviving institutional precedent, court rulings that still bind — supplies the framework within which Helm can coherently operate. As Archive depletes, the framework attenuates, and Helm's available scope of coherent action narrows even when its formal authority does not. The link is the slow constitutional substrate beneath visible governance. [e18]

edges.label:
Shocks spike coherence demand
+
Lag: Weeks
edges.description: External Shocks — economic crises, foreign-policy emergencies, climate events — produce immediate spikes in Coherence Demand as the population requires interpretation of what has happened and what is to be done. Shocks arriving in a high-Lore-Capacity environment are metabolised through ordinary deliberation. Shocks arriving in the current empty-pulpit configuration arrive into the vacuum and find whatever coherence-offer is closest to hand. [e19]

edges.label:
Renewal rebuilds narrative substrate
+
Lag: Decades
edges.description: Civic Renewal Capacity, when activated, directly rebuilds Lore Capacity through the patient work of replenishing the institutional substrate of shared narrative: durable civic organisations, restored press economics, reformed academic legitimacy, new shared-vocabulary projects. The lag is long because substrate-building always is, but the link is the principal recovery mechanism the diagram contains. [e20]

edges.label:
Shocks eventually force reckoning
+
Lag: Years
edges.description: Sufficiently severe External Shocks tend, eventually, to mobilise Civic Renewal Capacity that lower-intensity conditions cannot. The 1930s Depression, the 1960s civil rights moment, the post-Watergate reform period each followed this pattern. Shock-driven renewal arrives late and at high cost, but it does arrive — provided the institutional architecture required to host it has not been so depleted that hosting becomes impossible. [e21]

edges.label:
Unmet demand eventually mobilises citizens
+
Lag: Years
edges.description: Sustained unmet Coherence Demand — narrative hunger that has not been satisfied by Symbolic Acts or institutional Lore — eventually mobilises Civic Renewal Capacity directly through the lived experience of the empty pulpit becoming unbearable. This is the slow demand-side pathway to renewal that does not require an external shock. The lag is years rather than decades because the demand pressure is already present and the activation threshold is lower than shock-driven mobilisation requires. [e22]

loops.name: The Decisionist Spiral
loops.description: Coherence Demand pulls Symbolic Acts from the executive, which build Decisionist Authority, which further erodes Lore Capacity, which raises Coherence Demand. The loop reinforces with each cycle, and the dataset shows it operating across the last decade. Once entered, this spiral cannot be exited by the executive that is performing it — only by a structural intervention from outside the executive-mass dyad. [R1]

loops.name: The Praetorian Self-Reinforcement
loops.description: Symbolic Acts expand Praetorian Reach, which suppresses Lore Capacity renewal, which raises Coherence Demand, which pulls further Symbolic Acts. Each cycle deepens the substitution of Shield for the narrative-producing nodes and reduces the conditions under which those nodes could be restored. This is the structural mechanism by which Praetorian configurations become metastable rather than self-correcting. [R2]

loops.name: Symbolic Relief Valve
loops.description: Coherence Demand pulls Symbolic Acts which temporarily relieve Coherence Demand. This is the balancing mechanism the current configuration depends on — and the reason decisionist governance persists rather than collapsing. The valve works, briefly, each time. The fact that it works is what allows the deeper R1 and R2 spirals to keep operating: visible short-term relief masks structural long-term erosion. [B1]

loops.name: The Archive Drawdown
loops.description: Decisionist Authority draws down Archive, which weakens the framing that supports Helm Coherence, which forces further reliance on Symbolic Acts that build more Decisionist Authority and draw more Archive. The dataset's clearest warning signal: this loop is reinforcing in both directions and is currently running downward. Each year the loop operates, the institutional capital required for any future recovery is reduced. [R3]

loops.name: The Polarisation Engine
loops.description: Symbolic Acts mobilise Counter-Narrative response, which further fragments Lore Capacity and draws down Archive through partisan use of institutional channels, which raises Coherence Demand and pulls more Symbolic Acts. The loop describes how oppositional mobilisation, however justified in its substance, structurally accelerates the empty-pulpit condition it opposes. The political logic is irresistible; the structural cost is severe. [R4]

loops.name: The Renewal Path
loops.description: Civic Renewal Capacity rebuilds Lore Capacity, which reduces Coherence Demand, which (through sustained unmet hunger over time) further mobilises Civic Renewal Capacity — but in the reverse direction: when Lore is recovering and demand is falling, the activation pressure for further renewal also falls. This is the leverage loop of the entire diagram. It operates slowly, requires deliberate civic action outside partisan structures, and is the only path the geometry permits for exiting the Praetorian configuration short of catastrophic shock. The loop is presently inactive in the unfavourable direction (e22 firing weakly because the activation threshold has not been crossed). [B2]

archetypes.name: Shifting the Burden
archetypes.description: Symbolic Acts operate as the symptomatic solution to the problem of governing without functioning Lore. The fundamental solution would be Civic Renewal rebuilding the Lore substrate over time, but the symptomatic solution's short-term effectiveness (B1) atrophies the conditions under which the fundamental solution could operate. The loops R1, R2, R3, R4, B1, and B2 jointly express this archetype: B1 is the working symptomatic relief, R1 through R4 are the side effects compounding across decades, and B2 is the fundamental solution path the system geometry preserves but does not currently activate.

archetypes.name: Drift to Low Performance
archetypes.description: The system geometry permits a slow ratcheting downward in which each cycle of the reinforcing loops establishes a new baseline that becomes the reference point for the next cycle. What would have been a constitutional crisis in 2010 becomes routine in 2026. What was unthinkable in 2020 becomes precedent in 2025. The drift operates through the interaction of R1, R2, R3, and R4 with the absent B2: as long as Civic Renewal Capacity remains unactivated, the reinforcing loops collectively establish ever-lower performance norms across all eight CAMS nodes except Shield.
